Title: The redox state of the apoplast influences the acclimation of photosynthesis and leaf metabolism to changing irradiance

Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray, ArrayExpress E-MTAB-4816; metabolite profiling by GC/MS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low light (250 μmol m−2 s−1); high light (1600 μmol m−2 s−1); transition from high light to low light; photoperiod time
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Altered AO activity changed apoplastic ascorbate/DHA redox state and threonate accumulation; low AO activity reduced susceptibility of photosynthesis to high-light inhibition
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: AO activity varied over the photoperiod; WT AO previously reported to show strong dark/light expression pattern
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study manipulated AO activity using antisense tobacco AO and heterologous pumpkin AO expression, but genotype effects on the transcriptome were much smaller than light effects. AO is treated here as a common gene because the paper emphasizes redox/apoplastic control rather than enzymatic pathway assignment for a nutrient end product.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Youngest fully expanded leaves; strongest physiological effects under high light and after transition from high light to low light
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic antisense suppression; physiological assays; apoplastic and whole-leaf ascorbate/DHA measurements; photosynthetic gas exchange; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Reduced tobacco AO activity in TAO lines lowered apoplastic oxidation and maintained higher photosynthetic carbon assimilation under high light. 2. AO activity strongly altered apoplastic ascorbate/DHA ratios and influenced threonate, amino acids and fatty acids during acclimation to changing irradiance. 3. AO activity varied during the photoperiod, indicating post-transcriptional regulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing AO activity may improve tolerance of photosynthesis to high irradiance and help stabilize metabolic acclimation in crops under fluctuating light.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ascorbate functions in antioxidant/redox buffering and photoprotection; threonate reflects ascorbate degradation status.
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: WT and transgenic T3 tobacco lines; cultivar not stated
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major pigment or leaf number penalties reported; altered amino acid and fatty acid profiles may accompany AO manipulation.

Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray, ArrayExpress E-MTAB-4816; metabolite profiling by GC/MS
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low light; high light; return from high light to low light; photoperiod time
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High PAO activity increased oxidation of the apoplastic ascorbate pool and increased threonate; associated with lower photosynthetic carbon assimilation than TAO under high light
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pumpkin AO transgene was used in sense orientation in tobacco; maximal extractable AO activity was up to ~25-fold higher than WT and varied strongly over the photoperiod despite 35S expression, supporting post-transcriptional regulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Activity highest around 6 h into photoperiod in leaves, then declined by end of photoperiod
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; AO activity assay; apoplastic ascorbate/DHA assay; GC/MS threonate analysis; photosynthesis measurements
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pumpkin AO overexpression strongly elevated AO activity in tobacco leaves. 2. High AO activity promoted a more oxidized apoplastic ascorbate pool and increased threonate accumulation. 3. PAO plants showed altered amino acid and fatty acid responses during acclimation to changing irradiance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a tool gene to manipulate apoplastic redox state, though elevated AO may reduce photosynthetic performance under high light.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Ascorbate supports antioxidant protection and redox signaling; threonate indicates ascorbate catabolism.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Associated with lower photosynthetic carbon assimilation under high light relative to low-AO plants; altered lipid and amino-acid remodeling.
